desiredRefs.title: Clear Filters resets active browse filters

summary: Current modal clear behavior appears local to the modal, while desired UX makes Clear Filters an active browse reset.

preserved: The user retains an explicit way to clear filters.
changed: Clear Filters changes from modal-local cleanup to confirmed active browse reset.
added: Confirmation is required before active filter constraints are reset.
removed: Clear Filters should not be treated as local-only cleanup in desired UX.
behaviorDelta.notesMd: This mapping is the decision surface; neither current nor desired rows should point to implementation files as governing authority.

recommendation.summary: Use the desired active-browse reset behavior after confirmation.
recommendation.rationaleMd: The label `Clear Filters` most naturally means clearing active filter constraints from the user's perspective.

alternatives.summary: Keep Clear Filters modal-local and introduce a separate active reset action.
alternatives.tradeoffsMd: This preserves current semantics but risks two similar actions and more user confusion.

recommendation.consequencesMd: This is a material behavior change and should not become governing until confirmed.

confirmation.notesMd: User confirmation is required before implementation can treat the desired row as governing.

confirmationBaseline.notesMd: Backfilled as the audit baseline for the one-pass UX spec creation/update. This records provenance only; it does not replace UX authority, product confirmation, behavior mappings, or runtime drift ledgers.
